FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class SelectorBar

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI SelectorBar: a segmented horizontal row of text (optionally icon+text) items. The selected item is marked by a SHORT CENTERED accent pill flush with the item bottom (not a full-width underline, not bold); text stays TextPrimary at rest for every item and DIMS on hover/press — backgrounds are transparent in every state (SelectorBar_themeresources.xaml:21-25). Stateless — the caller owns selected and reacts to onSelect. Keyboard: ONE tab stop (the selected item — TabNavigation=Once, SelectorBar.xaml:13); Left/Right/ Home/End move focus item-to-item and SELECTION FOLLOWS FOCUS (SelectorBarTests.cs:63-66); focus entering a bar without a selection auto-selects (SelectorBar.cpp:98-126). Per-part restyling goes through the optional parts (see TemplateParts for the contract).

public static class SelectorBar

SelectorBar

object.ToString()

Fields

PartItem

Each item's clickable box (WinUI SelectorBarItem). The SAME modifier runs for every item — branch on caller state for per-item styling. Owned: OnClick (select), Role, TabStop (roving stop), OnKeyDown, OnFocusChanged, OnRealized (handle capture, chained).

public const string PartItem = "Item"

Field Value

string

PartPill

The short centered selection pill under each item (mounted only on the selected item). Owned: nothing — pure styling.

public const string PartPill = "Pill"

Field Value

string

Methods

Create(IReadOnlyList<string>, int, Action<int>, TemplateParts?, IReadOnlyList<string?>?)

icons = optional per-item glyphs (WinUI SelectorBarItem.Icon, SelectorBar.idl): rendered before the text at 0.8 scale with the −2,0 icon margin, recolored by the same foreground states.

public static Element Create(IReadOnlyList<string> items, int selected, Action<int> onSelect, TemplateParts? parts = null, IReadOnlyList<string?>? icons = null)

items IReadOnlyList<string>
selected int
onSelect Action<int>
parts TemplateParts
icons IReadOnlyList<string>

Returns

Element
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
